title: Rooms for rent near CSU downtown Cleveland
metaTitle: Rooms for Rent Near Cleveland State University
metaDescription: Searching for a room for rent near Cleveland State University? See downtown transit, nearby neighborhoods, and rent ranges, then tell us what you need.
primaryKeyword: rooms for rent near cleveland state
answerBox: Rooms for rent near Cleveland State University are typically found in and around Downtown Cleveland and the adjacent Central and St. Clair-Superior areas, reachable from CSU's campus via RTA's Red Line, HealthLine, and downtown bus routes. Off-campus room rentals vary widely in price and condition; verify each listing's lease terms and roommate arrangement individually.

sections.h2: Where do off-campus rooms near CSU come from?
body: Cleveland State University (CSU) sits at the eastern edge of Downtown Cleveland, bordering the **Campus International District** and close to **Central** and **St. Clair-Superior**. Off-campus room rentals near CSU generally take one of two forms: a private bedroom within a shared house or apartment (roommate-style), or a single room within a larger rental home split among several tenants — both are distinct from a self-contained studio or one-bedroom apartment.

Because a room-share depends on the specific house or apartment, rent and lease terms (whether utilities are split, whether there's one master lease or individual room leases) vary property to property — always get the exact arrangement in writing before paying anything.

CSU's campus is served directly by Downtown Cleveland's transit hub. **Tower City**, where all of GCRTA's rail lines converge (the Red Line, plus the Blue, Green, and Waterfront light-rail lines), is a short ride or walk from campus, and the **HealthLine** bus-rapid-transit route runs along Euclid Avenue connecting Public Square, CSU's edge, and University Circle. An extensive regular bus network also serves downtown and the near-east neighborhoods around campus.

For renters without a car, proximity to the Red Line or a HealthLine stop is often the deciding factor between two otherwise similar rooms — worth checking against a specific address before committing.

sections.h2: What does a room near CSU typically cost?
body: Room-share pricing in and around downtown Cleveland is generally lower than renting a self-contained apartment, since the cost of a house or apartment is split among several tenants. For comparison, **Zumper's July 2026 report** puts Cleveland's overall studio median at **$1,060/month** and 1-bedroom at **$1,195/month** — a shared room in a multi-bedroom home is typically a fraction of a full unit's rent, though the split depends on the number of roommates and how utilities are handled.

rows: A fraction of a comparable unit's rent, split among roommates

sections.h2: What to check before signing on a shared room
body: A room-share near campus is only as reliable as the paperwork behind it. Ask whether you'll be added to the master lease (making you jointly responsible for the whole unit) or given a separate room-only agreement, how utilities are split among roommates, and whether the security deposit is refunded to you directly or pooled with the other tenants at move-out. Under **Ohio Revised Code 5321.16**, a landlord generally must return a departing tenant's deposit — with an itemized statement of any deductions — within 30 days of move-out; how that applies to a shared unit with several names on the lease is worth clarifying in writing before you move in. (Informational only, not legal advice; verify current law.)

It's also worth confirming who's responsible for finding a replacement roommate if someone leaves mid-lease, since that's a common friction point in shared student and resident housing near any urban campus, not just CSU.

sections.h2: If a self-contained rental fits better
body: Not every CSU-area renter wants a shared room — some prefer a full apartment or a small house instead, with a single lease and no roommate dependency. We work with 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, and every home we work with accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Our inventory is concentrated in East and Southeast Cleveland neighborhoods like Hough and Central, a short distance from CSU and downtown; [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll show you what's currently available.

You can also compare full [apartments for rent in Cleveland](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) or general [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) if a private unit with its own lease works better for your budget and living situation than a shared room.

faq.q: What neighborhoods are closest to Cleveland State University for renting a room?
faq.a: Downtown Cleveland itself, along with the adjacent Central and St. Clair-Superior areas just east and north of campus, are the closest neighborhoods. All are within walking distance or a short transit ride of CSU via Tower City's rail hub or the HealthLine on Euclid Avenue.

faq.q: How do I get to CSU without a car?
faq.a: GCRTA's Red Line and the HealthLine bus-rapid-transit route both serve Downtown Cleveland near CSU's campus, connecting to Tower City (the region's rail hub) and University Circle. An extensive regular bus network also covers the downtown and near-east side.

faq.q: Is a shared room cheaper than a full apartment near CSU?
faq.a: Generally yes — splitting a house or apartment's rent among multiple roommates typically costs less per person than renting a self-contained unit alone. Cleveland's studio median was $1,060/month and 1-bedroom $1,195/month per Zumper's July 2026 report; a shared room is usually a fraction of that, depending on the number of roommates.

faq.q: Do Section 8 vouchers work for a room in a shared house near CSU?
faq.a: Voucher use for a shared or room-rental arrangement depends on the specific lease structure and CMHA's rules for that unit type — it's more common with a full-unit lease. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with is a self-contained unit that accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready; contact us to discuss what fits your situation.
